info.title: App Connect CRM Server API
info.description: HTTP API for the App Connect CRM server. The contract covers shared routes from @app-connect/core and connector-specific routes in this server. Response payloads may include connector-defined fields where an operation delegates to a connector interface.

host.description: Hostname of the deployed App Connect server.

tags.name: Manifest
tags.description: Connector manifests, implemented interfaces, release notes, and version metadata.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: CRM authorization, managed-auth state, and user-session lifecycle.

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: Account administration, managed credentials, settings, and user mapping.

tags.name: User
tags.description: Per-user settings and connector user information.

tags.name: Call Logs
tags.description: CRM call-log lookup, creation, updates, notes, and dispositions.

tags.name: Message Logs
tags.description: CRM logging for SMS, fax, voicemail, and related messages.

tags.name: Call Down
tags.description: Call-down list creation, retrieval, updates, and deletion.

tags.name: Plugins
tags.description: Plugin registration, callbacks, license checks, and plugin execution.

tags.name: MCP
tags.description: Model Context Protocol discovery, OAuth metadata, RPC, and widget calls.

tags.name: RingCentral Reports
tags.description: RingCentral account and extension reporting endpoints.

tags.name: Google Integrations
tags.description: Google Sheets and Google Drive connector-specific flows.

tags.name: Pipedrive
tags.description: Pipedrive redirect and application lifecycle callbacks.

get.summary: List implemented connector methods for a platform

200.description: Connector-interface availability for the requested platform.

get.description: Deprecated legacy manifest endpoint retained for client compatibility.

get.summary: Return merged global and connector release notes

200.description: Release notes grouped by server version and connector.
Version metadata from the default CRM manifest.

get.description: Deprecated legacy version endpoint retained for client compatibility.

parameters.name: proxyId
parameters.description: Proxy connector identifier retained through the OAuth exchange.

post.description: Authenticates a user with connector-defined API-key credentials. The platform field selects the connector; credential fields can be supplied directly or resolved from managed authentication when a RingCentral access token is present.

200.description: The connector credentials were accepted and an App Connect user token was issued.

parameters.name: isPrivate
parameters.description: Whether connector-scoped managed credentials should be used.

200.description: Managed API-key field state for the RingCentral user and account.
Managed OAuth configuration state for the RingCentral account.

get.summary: Resolve hostname metadata for a platform

500.description: The hostname could not be loaded.

get.description: Returns the CRM hostname stored for the authenticated App Connect user.

get.summary: Return a hash for the current user info

parameters.name: isPrivate
parameters.description: Whether connector-scoped managed credentials should be used.

200.description: Managed authentication field definitions and configured account/user values.
The managed authentication values were updated.
The pending managed OAuth values were cached.
The pending managed OAuth values were removed.
The account managed OAuth values were reset.

get.summary: Preload user settings for a platform

parameters.name: rcAccountId
parameters.description: RingCentral account identifier used when no access token is available.

200.description: Account-level user settings to preload before CRM login.

post.description: Resolves the current user from the App Connect JWT, refreshes OAuth credentials when needed, then calls the connector's refreshUserInfo interface.

parameters.name: rcAccountId
parameters.description: RingCentral account id used to resolve account-managed defaults when an access token is unavailable.

200.description: Effective user settings after account-level overrides are applied.
Updated user settings. Legacy connector-hook failures can return an empty object.

empty.summary: Legacy connector hook returned no settings

parameters.name: overridingFormat
parameters.description: Comma-separated phone-number formats used by CRM-specific matching.

parameters.name: isForceRefreshAccountData
parameters.description: Bypass and refresh cached contact search data.

200.description: Appointments returned by the active connector.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Inclusive appointment start date. Supply together with endDate.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Inclusive appointment end date. Supply together with startDate.

returnMessage.message: Could not create appointment in Bullhorn.

post.description: Normalizes the supplied status to lowercase and delegates the update to the connector appointment interface.

200.description: Appointment status update result; connector support varies.

unsupported.summary: Connector does not support status changes

returnMessage.message: This connector does not support appointment status changes.

get.description: Looks up local App Connect call-log linkage records by RingCentral session id and extension identity. hashedExtensionId is preferred when present; extensionNumber is retained as a legacy fallback. When requireDetails is true, also fetches the current CRM activity details through the connector getCallLog interface.

created.summary: Call log created with a numeric connector id

post.description: Creates one CRM activity/log for a RingCentral call and stores the local mapping for later updates. The mapping is keyed by session id plus extension identity. Client-side requests may send hashedExtensionId; server-side logging may send rcExtensionId, which the adapter server hashes locally before duplicate checks and persistence. The logInfo object follows the RingCentral CallLogRecord shape, trimmed to fields consumed by App Connect.

patch.description: Updates an existing CRM call activity using the local mapping found by sessionId and extension identity. hashedExtensionId is preferred when present; rcExtensionId is hashed locally for server-side logging; extensionNumber remains a legacy fallback.

post.summary: Cache a call note before full logging
Create or update an SMS/fax/message log

created.summary: Message logs created with numeric connector ids

post.description: Creates or updates CRM activities for SMS, MMS, voicemail, fax, and shared SMS conversations. The server groups normal messages by conversation/day and shared SMS by conversationLogId.

post.summary: Register a plugin for an account

200.description: The plugin was registered for the account.

delete.summary: Unregister a plugin for an account

get.summary: Get plugin license status for a user

parameters.name: rcAccountId
parameters.description: RingCentral account id whose plugin license is being checked.

parameters.name: pluginId
parameters.description: Plugin identifier from the connector manifest.

post.description: Receives async call-log plugin completion. The task id in the path validates the callback task.

404.description: Async plugin task was not found or expired.

500.description: Callback was valid, but App Connect failed to update the CRM log.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: IANA timezone used to group report data.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: IANA timezone used for report data.

get.description: Exchanges the RingCentral OAuth authorization code for the account associated with the authenticated App Connect user.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: One-time authorization code returned by RingCentral OAuth.

200.description: The RingCentral OAuth authorization was completed.

get.summary: OAuth authorization server metadata for MCP PKCE clients
Redirect MCP PKCE OAuth authorization to RingCentral

302.description: Redirect to the RingCentral OAuth authorization endpoint.

500.description: The MCP OAuth client is not configured.

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: MCP public client identifier. When supplied, it must match the configured client.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Opaque OAuth state returned to the client.

post.summary: Return MCP public client registration metadata

401.description: An authenticated MCP method requires a RingCentral OAuth token. The response includes WWW-Authenticate metadata.

post.description: Handles MCP JSON-RPC requests. Discovery and handshake methods are public; tool calls that access user data require a RingCentral bearer token.

404.description: The requested widget tool is not registered.

post.description: Executes an allowlisted widget tool after validating the signed widget-session header.

post.summary: Create or connect a Google Sheet

get.summary: Open admin Google Sheets file picker

post.summary: Create or connect an admin Google Sheet
Select an admin Google Sheet tab

parameters.name: pluginId
parameters.description: Plugin identifier included in OAuth state.

parameters.name: callbackUri
parameters.description: Original OAuth callback URI containing encoded state.

post.description: Legacy endpoint that accepts the App Connect session JWT in the request body.

500.description: The redirect helper page could not be served.
The Pipedrive user could not be disconnected.

userJwt.description: App Connect user-session JWT. A response may rotate this token in the X-Refreshed-JWT-Token header.

legacyUserJwt.name: jwtToken
legacyUserJwt.description: Legacy App Connect user-session JWT query parameter. Prefer Authorization: Bearer.

ringCentralAccessToken.name: X-RC-Access-Token
ringCentralAccessToken.description: RingCentral OAuth access token used for user or administrator validation.

legacyRingCentralAccessToken.name: rcAccessToken
legacyRingCentralAccessToken.description: Legacy RingCentral access-token query parameter. Prefer X-RC-Access-Token. Some legacy request bodies also carry rcAccessToken.

mcpBearer.description: RingCentral OAuth access token used by authenticated MCP methods.

widgetSessionToken.name: X-App-Connect-Widget-Token
widgetSessionToken.description: Short-lived signed session token issued to the App Connect MCP widget.

filePickerJwt.name: token
filePickerJwt.description: Legacy App Connect user-session JWT used by the Google Sheets picker page.

pipedriveBasicAuth.description: Pipedrive application credentials used to authenticate uninstall callbacks.

phoneNumber.name: phoneNumber
phoneNumber.description: Phone number to search, normally E.164.

taskId.name: taskId
taskId.description: Async plugin task id generated by App Connect.

sessionIds.name: sessionIds
sessionIds.description: Comma-separated RingCentral call session ids. The server processes at most five ids.

extensionNumber.name: extensionNumber
extensionNumber.description: Legacy RingCentral extension number used to disambiguate duplicated call session ids when hashedExtensionId is unavailable.

hashedExtensionId.name: hashedExtensionId
hashedExtensionId.description: Hashed RingCentral extension id used to disambiguate duplicated call session ids. Preferred over extensionNumber when available.

requireDetails.name: requireDetails
requireDetails.description: When true, the server calls the connector getCallLog interface and includes CRM log details.

isExtension.name: isExtension
isExtension.description: Marks the lookup as an internal extension-number lookup.

GenericJson.description: Connector-defined JSON payload. The exact fields depend on the selected connector interface.

ApiKeyLogin.description: Connector and credential data used to create an App Connect user session.

CallDisposition.description: Call-log identity and the dispositions selected by the user.

AppointmentStatusUpdate.description: New appointment status. Status support depends on connector capability.

PluginRegister.description: Plugin and RingCentral account identifiers to register.

UserSettingsUpdate.description: User settings to upsert and setting keys to remove.

AdminSettingsUpdate.description: Account-level App Connect settings. Nested setting definitions are extensible.

ManagedAuthUpdate.description: Account-scoped or user-scoped managed authentication values.

AdminManagedOAuthCache.description: Managed OAuth values cached until the account OAuth flow completes.

AppointmentCreate.description: Appointment payload. The wrapper is canonical; the direct form remains for compatibility.

AppointmentPatch.description: Partial appointment update. Omitted fields are preserved.

OkJson.description: Successful JSON response. Exact response shape depends on the route and connector interface.

BadRequest.description: Bad request, missing authentication, or connector error.

Forbidden.description: The caller is authenticated but is not permitted to perform the operation.

UnauthorizedSession.description: The user session is expired or revoked.

InternalServerError.description: The server could not complete the operation because of an unexpected internal or upstream error.

messageType.description: Presentation severity. Common values are success, info, warning, danger, and error.

platform.description: Connector platform identifier from the App Connect manifest.

apiKey.description: Primary connector credential. It can be omitted when managed authentication supplies the value.

rcAccessToken.description: Legacy body location for a RingCentral access token. Use the X-RC-Access-Token header instead.

hostname.description: Optional connector host name for CRM deployments with tenant-specific endpoints.

connectorId.description: Developer Portal connector identifier used to resolve managed authentication fields.

isPrivate.description: Whether connectorId identifies a private connector.

additionalInfo.description: Connector-defined credential fields, such as tenant or user identifiers.

jwtToken.description: App Connect JWT used to authenticate subsequent user operations.

name.description: Display name returned by the CRM connector.

ErrorResponse.description: Error payload. Legacy routes may return a plain string instead.

GenericSuccessResponse.description: Connector-defined JSON response. The exact fields depend on the selected connector interface.

AdditionalSubmission.description: Connector-defined custom fields collected by the App Connect client.

extensionNumber.description: Legacy RingCentral extension number identity fallback.

hashedExtensionId.description: Hashed RingCentral extension id generated by the client for client-side call logging requests.

rcExtensionId.description: Raw RingCentral extension id sent by server-side call logging; the adapter server hashes it locally before duplicate checks and persistence.

extensionNumber.description: Legacy RingCentral extension number identity fallback.

hashedExtensionId.description: Hashed RingCentral extension id used to find the local call-log mapping.

rcExtensionId.description: Raw RingCentral extension id sent by server-side call logging; the adapter server hashes it locally before lookup.

hashedExtensionId.description: Hashed RingCentral extension id used to find the local call-log mapping.

additionalSubmission.description: Accepted for client compatibility; the current call-disposition handler does not persist or forward these values.

toolArgs.description: Arguments accepted by the selected widget tool.

version.description: Version declared by the default CRM manifest.

extensionId.description: SHA-256 hash of the supplied extension identifier.

accountId.description: SHA-256 hash of the supplied account identifier.

presignedUrl.description: Short-lived URL for uploading the debug report.

pluginId.description: Plugin identifier from the developer portal.

rcAccountId.description: RingCentral account that will receive the plugin.

ownerRcAccountId.description: RingCentral account that owns a shared plugin.

pluginAccess.description: Plugin access mode, such as private, shared, or public.

rcAccessToken.description: Legacy body location for a RingCentral access token. Use the X-RC-Access-Token header instead.

UserSettings.description: User-setting entries keyed by their manifest setting identifier.

type.description: Release-note category such as New or Fix.

startTimeUtc.description: ISO-8601 appointment timestamp with a UTC offset.
ISO-8601 appointment timestamp with a UTC offset.

startTime.description: ISO-8601 appointment timestamp with a UTC offset.

description.description: Canonical appointment description returned by list operations.

summary.description: Compatibility alias returned by some create, update, and refresh connectors.

startTimeUtc.description: ISO-8601 appointment timestamp with a UTC offset.

attendeeIds.description: Compatibility representation returned by connectors that do not provide attendee objects.

RefreshedUserJwt.description: Rotated App Connect user-session JWT. Clients should replace the token they sent when this header is present.
